title: Picotia littoralis
intro: Picotia littoralis, also known as Picrorhiza, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the gentian family (Gentianaceae) and traditionally used as a medicinal herb. The health benefits of Picotia littoralis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve liver function, and act as a natural antioxidant. The bioactive constituents of this plant include icariin, isovitexin, and picroliv, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Picotia littoralis is commonly prepared as a root extract, capsule, or tea to treat various health conditions, and is also used to support digestion and immune function.
taxonomy: Picotia littoralis is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It belongs to the genus Picotia, which is a small genus of annual or biennial plants. The genus Picotia is part of the larger family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This family contains over 338 genera and more than 4,000 species of flowering plants. Picotia littoralis is a species within this large and diverse family.
